changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for consistency with existing patterns
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the NIST 800-53 AU-8-b control, focusing on audit logging and timestamp recording. However, the naming of 'k8s_audit_logging_enabled' was adjusted to 'k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled' to maintain consistency with the existing naming pattern that specifies the resource more clearly. The other function names are descriptive and follow the established naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource names are specific, e.g., use 'apiserver' for audit logging related functions
title: Time Stamps - Record time stamps for audit...
step1_reasoning: The NIST 800-53 AU-8-b control requires recording timestamps for audit logs, which directly maps to Kubernetes audit logging capabilities. Kubernetes can be configured to enable audit logging, ensure retention of logs, and capture detailed metadata including timestamps in audit policies. These configurations ensure compliance with the requirement to record time stamps for audit purposes.
